Bitcoin has consolidated within a $62.8K–$72.6K range for over a month and has failed to break out to the upside on multiple occasions.

Is the current move toward the range high the one that finally pushes BTC higher?

Gamma Concentration Above Spot

Gamma exposure reinforces that level. Roughly $4B of negative gamma sits near $75K, with $2.7B expiring at the end of March.

If BTC pushes into this region, dealer hedging flows could accelerate the move higher.

glassno.de/4bFCeEq

Wrap Up

Front-end IV is easing.

Skew still favors puts.

Bullish flow is building.

Put/call flows favor calls.

$75K strike is key.

Short gamma sits above.

Options positioning is gradually shifting from defensive hedging toward cautious upside exposure.

claude code can automate your ENTIRE cold outreach system & book you 30-40+ calls MONTHLY

but most of you don't even know how to set it up properly

so i'm giving away a detailed 54-page doc on EXACTLY this

like + comment “CLAUDE” and i'll send it over asap

(must follow + RT

treating appointment setting like sales is the DUMBEST mistake anyone can make

you're trying to close deals over dm when your only job is getting them on the phone

i’ve watched setters fail by pitching full service trying to convince prospects before call even happens

getting any reply is way more valuable than trying to book calls in your opener

this is because sunk cost fallacy makes people feel obligated to continue conversations they've already invested time in

i even tested this with 50,000+ cold messages, and the opener asking for call

the BIGGEST issue i see with using content for outreach is when you sound laid back and insightful

but then your setter makes you sound desperate and salesy, this is even worse if you have a bad closer too

you're making prospects get confused and ghost instead of buying by

every extra click or action in your outreach flow kills 20-30%+ of potential conversions through law of least effort

i know this because i watched response rates jump 3x purely from removing friction points prospects didn't consciously notice, but subconsciously resisted

and

cold outreach response rate has NOTHING to do with the message quality

i watched identical outreaches generate 20%+ response rates on different accounts

the only difference was WHAT prospects found when they googled the sender's name

look at what happens when your cold message

subject lines and email body copy are overrated

everyone stresses over them while completely ignoring preview text which determines 60-70% of open decisions

i've tested this with 500,000+ cold emails and found that preview text matters more than subject line for getting

95% of n8n users never deploy a single workflow to production.

Not because it's hard.

Because nobody shows them the 3-step activation shortcut:

→ Describe outcomes, not nodes
→ Pick ONE automation you'll actually use tomorrow
→ Write it in plain English first, build second

5 n8n workflows every agency should have running (and the one sentence to build each):

→ Lead intake → qualify with AI → CRM → notify sales
→ Overdue invoice → chase email → update status → follow-up task
→ New client signed → onboard sequence → Slack channel →

After: 11 minutes. Same workflow. Running in production.

Web3 Infrastructure Playnance Will Hold GCOIN Initial Listing on March 18

Playnance will launch $GCOIN on March 18. The $GCOIN powers its on-chain gaming, prediction, and financial platforms, already held by 200,000+ users with ~13B tokens distributed in presale

$GCOIN acts as the unified economic layer across Playnance’s ecosystem, enabling gameplay, predictions, settlements, and rewards. It runs on PlayBlock, the company’s fast, gasless blockchain, allowing non-custodial ownership and smooth on-chain interactions

The ecosystem recently generated $5.3M in revenue and $2M in cash payouts via its Be The Boss incentive. Founded in 2020, Playnance builds non-custodial Web3 platforms simplifying blockchain for mainstream users, processing ~2M transactions daily
